title: ipv6_addr_to_str snprintf truncation not detected on exact fit
description:
Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/ipv6_addr.c:169-211 (lichen_ipv6_addr_to_str)

Bug: The function checks if snprintf returns < 0 (encoding error) but does not check for truncation. snprintf returns the number of characters that WOULD have been written, not counting the null terminator. If buflen is exactly 40 (LICHEN_IPV6_ADDR_STR_LEN), and the format produces exactly 39 characters + null, ret will be 39, which is >= 0 but the output is correct.

However, if buflen were 39 (less than required), snprintf would return 39 (needed chars), ret would be >= 0, and the function would return 0 (success) even though the output was truncated.

The precondition check at line 176 catches buflen < LICHEN_IPV6_ADDR_STR_LEN, so in practice truncation cannot occur. But the snprintf return value check at line 202 is incomplete by snprintf semantics.

Impact: Low - precondition prevents the truncation case. But defensive coding would check ret >= buflen for truncation.

Fix: Add after line 202: if ((size_t)ret >= buflen) { LOG_ERR(...); return -ENOSPC; }
